name: Diarmuid
description: Diarmuid Ua Duibhne, often anglicized as Dermot O'Dyna, is a prominent hero in Irish mythology, particularly known from the Fenian Cycle. A skilled warrior and a loyal member of the Fianna, the elite band of warriors led by Fionn mac Cumhaill, Diarmuid is famed for his unparalleled beauty and charm, as well as his prowess in battle. He is most famously known for his tragic love affair with Gráinne, the betrothed of Fionn mac Cumhaill. Gráinne placed a geis (compulsion) upon Diarmuid to elope with her, leading to a lengthy and perilous flight across Ireland, pursued by Fionn and his warriors. Throughout their journey, Diarmuid displayed exceptional bravery and resourcefulness, evading capture and protecting Gráinne. Though he eventually reconciled with Fionn, the seeds of resentment remained. Diarmuid's fate was sealed when he was tricked into hunting a magical boar, which mortally wounded him. Fionn, possessing the power to heal Diarmuid with water from his hands, deliberately allowed the water to slip through his fingers, ultimately leading to Diarmuid's death. Diarmuid is remembered as a symbol of youthful beauty, loyalty, and tragic love, his story serving as a cautionary tale about the consequences of geasa and the enduring power of jealousy and betrayal. His legend continues to resonate in Irish folklore and literature, solidifying his place as one of the most captivating figures in Celtic mythology. He represents the ideals of courage, honor, and the devastating consequences of broken oaths and unrequited love.
subtitle: The Fair-Faced Warrior of the Fianna
achievements: Maintaining his honor despite Gráinne's geis
Being a loyal member of the Fianna

keyMyths.title: The Elopement with Gráinne
keyMyths.description: Gráinne, betrothed to Fionn mac Cumhaill, places a geis on Diarmuid to run away with her. This leads to a long chase across Ireland, testing Diarmuid's loyalty and skill.
keyMyths.source: The Pursuit of Diarmuid and Gráinne

keyMyths.title: Diarmuid's Death
keyMyths.description: Diarmuid is mortally wounded by an enchanted boar during a hunt, orchestrated by Fionn. Fionn, capable of healing him, allows him to die out of jealousy.
keyMyths.source: The Pursuit of Diarmuid and Gráinne

keyMyths.title: Diarmuid's Beauty Spot
keyMyths.description: Diarmuid possessed a magical beauty spot that made him irresistible to women. This beauty spot played a key role in Gráinne's decision to flee with him.

sources.author: Nora Chadwick
sources.description: Provides historical and cultural context for understanding Celtic mythology.
sources.text: Provides historical and cultural context for understanding Celtic mythology.

sources.author: Lady Gregory
